mission.title: Install and Configure Bpfman on Kubernetes
mission.description: bpfman is an eBPF Manager designed to simplify the deployment and management of eBPF programs in Kubernetes environments. Installing bpfman allows you to gain insights into eBPF utilization and manage eBPF programs effectively.

steps.title: Clone the bpfman repository
steps.description:
Start by cloning the bpfman repository to your local machine using the following command:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/bpfman/bpfman.git
cd bpfman
```

steps.title: Apply the Kubernetes manifests
steps.description:
Deploy bpfman to your Kubernetes cluster by applying the provided manifests:
```bash
kubectl apply -f deploy/kubernetes/namespace.yaml
kubectl apply -f deploy/kubernetes/bpfman.yaml --namespace bpfman --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the bpfman pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace bpfman
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the bpfman deployment to set resource limits. Use the following command to open the deployment in your editor:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment bpfman --namespace bpfman
``` 
Add resource limits under the containers section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
To access the bpfman service, set up port forwarding:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/bpfman --namespace bpfman 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the bpfman deployment
uninstall.description:
Delete the bpfman deployment and service:
```bash
kubectl delete -f deploy/kubernetes/bpfman.yaml --namespace bpfman
```

uninstall.title: Delete the bpfman namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the namespace created for bpfman:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace bpfman
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and state of bpfman:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace bpfman -o yaml > bpfman-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Pull the latest version
upgrade.description:
Fetch the latest version of bpfman from the repository:
```bash
git pull origin main
```

upgrade.title: Apply the updated manifests
upgrade.description:
Reapply the updated Kubernetes manifests to upgrade bpfman:
```bash
kubectl apply -f deploy/kubernetes/bpfman.yaml --namespace bpfman
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the upgraded bpfman pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace bpfman
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the bpfman pods are not starting, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace bpfman
``` 
Look for any error messages that indicate what might be wrong.

troubleshooting.title: Service not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the bpfman service, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly. Check the service configuration:
```bash
kubectl get svc bpfman --namespace bpfman -o yaml
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource allocation issues, check the resource limits set on the deployment:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment bpfman --namespace bpfman
``` 
Adjust the resource limits as necessary.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of bpfman will result in the bpfman pods running without errors, and you will be able to access the service via port forwarding. You should also have configured resource limits for optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f deploy/kubernetes/namespace.yaml
kubectl apply -f deploy/kubernetes/bpfman.yaml --namespace bpfman --create-namespace

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to interact with it.
